question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Block() request for a block with transactions containing operations that all reference the same account ID, causing the entityCache to be populated with one entry but GetAccountAlias() to still be called once per unique account, and if the cache is full, evicting useful entries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Block() request for a block with a very large number of transactions, causing the slice operation block.Transactions[0:s.maxTransactionsInBlock] at line 66 to retain a reference to the full underlying array, preventing garbage collection of the large transaction slice and causing memory leaks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockTransaction() request with a transaction hash that is an extremely long string (e.g., megabytes), which is passed without validation to FindByHashInBlock() at line 87, causing excessive memory allocation in the DB query layer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent Block() requests for the same high-transaction-count block, causing multiple goroutines to simultaneously execute FindBetween() and updateOperationAccountAlias(), multiplying memory usage proportionally to concurrency and exhausting heap?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Block() request with a PartialBlockIdentifier.Hash set to a string of all zeros or a known genesis hash, causing RetrieveBlock() to always return the genesis block (which may have many transactions), and then FindBetween() to load all genesis-era transactions into memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Block() request for a block index of 0 (genesis block), causing RetrieveBlock() to call FindByIndex(0) which may trigger a full table scan or return a block with an unusually large number of transactions, leading to memory exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockTransaction() request with a block index of math.MinInt64 or math.MaxInt64, causing FindByIdentifier() to execute a DB query with an extreme int64 value that triggers unexpected behavior or a full table scan?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Block() request for a block with a single transaction containing thousands of operations, causing the inner loop at lines 110-125 to iterate thousands of times and issue thousands of GetAccountAlias() DB calls in a single request, exhausting DB connections?
